da396b37db70288b26221b1675a64700ae448449.title: Improved Information Order
da396b37db70288b26221b1675a64700ae448449.body:
For every \(m\in\mathbb N\) with \(3\le m\), at truncation order \(L=2m+1\):
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Forward generic separation.)} There is a proper real algebraic subset \(E^{\mathrm{right}}\) of the real forward parameter space whose complement meets \(F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\), and for every \(\theta\in F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\setminus E^{\mathrm{right}}\) there is no \(\eta\in F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\) such that
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(\theta)=\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(\eta).
\]
\item \textbf{(Reverse generic separation.)} There is a proper real algebraic subset \(E^{\mathrm{left}}\) of the real reverse parameter space whose complement meets \(F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\), and for every \(\eta\in F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\setminus E^{\mathrm{left}}\) there is no \(\theta\in F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\) such that
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(\eta)=\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(\theta).
\]
\end{itemize}
Consequently, for the information order \(K^\star(m)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:information-order},
\[
K^\star(m)\le 2m+1
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
K^\star(m)\ne 2m+2 .
\]

8d9b0c18f03751f587eac72bd9de5764ac09c1fe.title: Generic Fiber Obstruction
8d9b0c18f03751f587eac72bd9de5764ac09c1fe.body:
For every integer \(m\ge 1\), set \(K=2m+2\).  There exist complex loci
\(U^{\mathrm{right}}_m\subseteq \Theta^{\mathrm{right},\circ}_{m,K}\) and
\(U^{\mathrm{left}}_m\subseteq \Theta^{\mathrm{left},\circ}_{m,K}\), with the generic loci as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:generic-parameter-loci}, such that:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Relative genericity.)}  Each of \(U^{\mathrm{right}}_m\) and \(U^{\mathrm{left}}_m\) is relatively Zariski open and relatively Zariski dense in the retained-band complex parameter space at order \(K\).

\item \textbf{(Real incidence.)}  There is a Euclidean-open set of real forward parameters whose intersection with \(F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\) from Definition~\ref{obj:def:real-feasible-regions} is nonempty and whose complexification is contained in \(U^{\mathrm{right}}_m\).  Likewise, there is a Euclidean-open set of real reverse parameters whose intersection with \(F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\) is nonempty and whose complexification is contained in \(U^{\mathrm{left}}_m\).

\item \textbf{(Forward generic fibers.)}  For every \(\theta=(\gamma,\rho,c)\in U^{\mathrm{right}}_m\), every forward parameter in the forward fiber
\[
R^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\!\left(\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}(\theta)\right)
\]
of Definition~\ref{obj:def:fiber-correspondences} and Definition~\ref{obj:def:forward-cumulant-map} has the same unordered loading-slope multiset
\[
\{\gamma,\rho_1,\ldots,\rho_m\}
\]
as \(\theta\).  The opposite-arrow fiber
\[
R^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\!\left(\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}(\theta)\right)
\]
of Definition~\ref{obj:def:reverse-cumulant-map} is empty.  For every latent index \(i=1,\ldots,m\), there is a generic forward parameter \(\theta'\in\Theta^{\mathrm{right},\circ}_{m,K}\) obtained by interchanging the complete forward direct-source pair with latent source \(i\), such that
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}(\theta')
=
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}(\theta),
\]
but \(\theta'\) is not in the admissible \(G_m\)-orbit of \(\theta\).  If \(m\ge 2\), this forward fiber has exact relative Zariski dimension
\[
\frac{m(m-1)}{2}.
\]

\item \textbf{(Reverse generic fibers.)}  For every \(\eta=(\delta,\sigma,d)\in U^{\mathrm{left}}_m\), every reverse parameter in the reverse fiber
\[
R^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\!\left(\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}(\eta)\right)
\]
has the same unordered loading-slope multiset
\[
\{\delta,\sigma_1,\ldots,\sigma_m\}
\]
as \(\eta\).  The opposite-arrow fiber
\[
R^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\!\left(\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}(\eta)\right)
\]
is empty.  For every latent index \(i=1,\ldots,m\), there is a generic reverse parameter \(\eta'\in\Theta^{\mathrm{left},\circ}_{m,K}\) obtained by interchanging the complete reverse direct-source pair with latent source \(i\), such that
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}(\eta')
=
\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}(\eta),
\]
but \(\eta'\) is not in the admissible \(G_m\)-orbit of \(\eta\).  If \(m\ge 2\), this reverse fiber has exact relative Zariski dimension
\[
\frac{m(m-1)}{2}.
\]
\end{itemize}

aa7c1bb9d4e7c940dfc39f0f1a8423d43c3c6f17.title: Admissible Swaps Preserve Direction
aa7c1bb9d4e7c940dfc39f0f1a8423d43c3c6f17.body:
Let \(m\ge 1\), let \(2\le L\le 2m+2\), and let \(\pi\in G_m\).  Let
\(\theta^{\mathbb C}\in\Theta^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\) and
\(\eta^{\mathbb C}\in\Theta^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\) be complex parameters, let
\(\theta\in F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\) and
\(\eta\in F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\) be real parameters when feasibility is assumed, and let
\(P_{\mathrm{right}},P_{\mathrm{left}}\in\operatorname{Laws}(\mathbb R^2)\).  Then:

\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Complex cumulant maps.)} The admissible source relabeling of
Definition~\ref{obj:def:admissible-source-swaps} leaves both complex cumulant maps unchanged:
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(\pi\theta^{\mathbb C})
  =\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(\theta^{\mathbb C}),
\qquad
\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(\pi\eta^{\mathbb C})
  =\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(\eta^{\mathbb C}).
\]

\item \textbf{(Real feasible regions.)} If \(\theta\in F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\), then
\(\pi\theta\in F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\); and if
\(\eta\in F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\), then
\(\pi\eta\in F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\), with feasibility understood as in
Definition~\ref{obj:def:real-feasible-regions}.

\item \textbf{(Forward witnesses.)} For any probability space satisfying the usual
measurability and integrability conditions, any centered independent non-Gaussian
sources \(S_0,\ldots,S_{m+1}\) satisfying
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:independent-sources},
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:source-nongaussianity}, and
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:finite-cumulants}, and any observed pair \((X,Y)\) with
law \(P_{\mathrm{right}}\), suppose \(\theta\in F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\), the
forward axis model of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:forward-axis-model} holds with
parameter \(\theta\), the conditions of
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:forward-noncollinearity} and
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:forward-nonzero-edge} hold, and
\[
\operatorname{Cum}_r(S_j)=c_{jr}
\qquad\text{for every }j\text{ and every }2\le r\le L .
\]
After relabeling the middle sources by \(\pi\), the relabeled sources remain
independent, centered, non-Gaussian, and finite through order \(2m+2\); they satisfy
the same forward axis model with parameter \(\pi\theta\), the forward
noncollinearity and nonzero-edge conditions still hold, and their cumulants satisfy
\[
\operatorname{Cum}_r(S_{\pi(j)})=(\pi c)_{jr}
\qquad\text{for every }j\text{ and every }2\le r\le L .
\]
Consequently \(P_{\mathrm{right}}\in\mathcal P^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\) as in
Definition~\ref{obj:def:forward-lvlingam-class}.

\item \textbf{(Reverse witnesses.)} The analogous reverse statement holds: for any
reverse representation of \(P_{\mathrm{left}}\) satisfying
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:independent-sources},
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:source-nongaussianity},
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:finite-cumulants},
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:reverse-axis-model},
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:reverse-noncollinearity}, and
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:reverse-nonzero-edge}, with
\(\eta\in F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\) and
\[
\operatorname{Cum}_r(S_j)=d_{jr}
\qquad\text{for every }j\text{ and every }2\le r\le L ,
\]
the relabeled sources remain valid reverse witnesses with parameter \(\pi\eta\),
their cumulants satisfy
\[
\operatorname{Cum}_r(S_{\pi(j)})=(\pi d)_{jr}
\qquad\text{for every }j\text{ and every }2\le r\le L ,
\]
and \(P_{\mathrm{left}}\in\mathcal P^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\) as in
Definition~\ref{obj:def:reverse-lvlingam-class}.

\item \textbf{(Direction of structural models.)} If a structural model \(M\) has
\(D(M)=X\to Y\) and parameter \(\theta\), then there is a structural model \(M'\)
with \(P_{M'}=P_M\), \(D(M')=X\to Y\), and parameter \(\pi\theta\).  If \(M\) has
\(D(M)=Y\to X\) and parameter \(\eta\), then there is a structural model \(M'\)
with \(P_{M'}=P_M\), \(D(M')=Y\to X\), and parameter \(\pi\eta\).

\item \textbf{(Arrow-tagged orbits.)} On complex parameters, the relabeled source
weights are exactly the permuted weights:
\[
(\pi c^{\mathbb C})_{jr}=c^{\mathbb C}_{\pi(j),r},
\qquad
(\pi d^{\mathbb C})_{jr}=d^{\mathbb C}_{\pi(j),r}.
\]
For the boundary source labels \(j=0\) and \(j=m+1\), the forward loading vectors
are fixed by the admissible relabeling.  Moreover every admissible relabeling
preserves the arrow tag: right-tagged parameters remain right-tagged and
left-tagged parameters remain left-tagged.  Hence the right-tagged \(G_m\)-orbit of
\(\theta^{\mathbb C}\) is disjoint from the left-tagged \(G_m\)-orbit of
\(\eta^{\mathbb C}\).

\item \textbf{(All admissible relabelings.)} For every \(\sigma\in G_m\),
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(\sigma\theta^{\mathbb C})
  =\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(\theta^{\mathbb C}),
\qquad
\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(\sigma\eta^{\mathbb C})
  =\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(\eta^{\mathbb C}).
\]
\end{itemize}

c12f522324433f0c837e35e9795f3f16ddafa9b7.title: Independent latent sources
c12f522324433f0c837e35e9795f3f16ddafa9b7.body:
The centered latent source variables \(S_0,\ldots,S_{m+1}\) are mutually independent:
\[
S_0\perp\cdots\perp S_{m+1}.
\]

3979afbc32ef4f9baaee7370d97afd1c6639e568.title: Finite source cumulants
3979afbc32ef4f9baaee7370d97afd1c6639e568.body:
For every \(j=0,\ldots,m+1\), the latent source variable \(S_j\) has finite \(K\)-th absolute moment:
\[
\mathbb E |S_j|^K<\infty .
\]

4a16089ceaeca7cedce1d648d0cd9448a6dfb452.title: Non-Gaussian sources
4a16089ceaeca7cedce1d648d0cd9448a6dfb452.body: For every \(j=0,\ldots,m+1\), the latent source variable \(S_j\) is non-Gaussian.

e6fd617dff69318fb5510369be09a98cb991a9de.title: Forward axis representation
e6fd617dff69318fb5510369be09a98cb991a9de.body:
The bivariate observed outcome vector admits the forward latent linear representation
\[
(X,Y)^\top=\sum_{j=0}^{m+1} u_j S_j .
\]

e5011f62d83eed0ae19f6fbe064c742733ef795e.title: Reverse axis representation
e5011f62d83eed0ae19f6fbe064c742733ef795e.body:
The bivariate observed outcome vector admits the reverse latent linear representation
\[
(X,Y)^\top=\sum_{j=0}^{m+1} v_j S_j .
\]

ea7362c8dfdc131c3bc3bc84fc1cebf4cc6be443.title: Distinct forward slopes
ea7362c8dfdc131c3bc3bc84fc1cebf4cc6be443.body:
The direct and latent finite slopes in the forward parametrization are pairwise distinct:
\[
\bigl|\{\gamma,\rho_i:1\le i\le m\}\bigr|=m+1 .
\]

384985cb7abb170b5549359bda21ee725d4741a0.title: Reverse slope separation
384985cb7abb170b5549359bda21ee725d4741a0.body:
The reverse slopes are pairwise distinct:
\[
\left|\{\delta,\sigma_i:1\le i\le m\}\right|=m+1.
\]

21f700dc18e66ca5d6e649057b08a44ec1fc5049.title: Nonzero forward edge
21f700dc18e66ca5d6e649057b08a44ec1fc5049.body:
The forward direct slope is nonzero:
\[
\gamma\ne 0.
\]

3a64f62deb8e6a86eb082f621fa0f56c277117cf.title: Nonzero reverse edge
3a64f62deb8e6a86eb082f621fa0f56c277117cf.body:
The reverse direct slope is nonzero:
\[
\delta\ne 0.
\]

962997cfe36184340af8f6e91e3d3be5cbcd49c1.title: Forward class \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\)
962997cfe36184340af8f6e91e3d3be5cbcd49c1.body:
Let \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\) be the set of all probability laws \(P\in \operatorname{Laws}(\mathbb R^2)\) for which there exist centered latent source variables
\[
S_0,\ldots,S_{m+1}
\]
such that:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Source independence.)} The sources \(S_0,\ldots,S_{m+1}\) are mutually independent.
\item \textbf{(Finite cumulant order.)} Each source has finite moments through order \(K\).
\item \textbf{(Source non-Gaussianity.)} Each source is non-Gaussian.
\item \textbf{(Forward representation.)} The bivariate observed outcome vector \((X,Y)^\top\) admits the forward \(X\to Y\) latent linear non-Gaussian representation with loading vectors \(u_j\).
\item \textbf{(Forward noncollinearity.)} The forward direct and latent finite slopes \(\gamma,\rho_1,\ldots,\rho_m\) satisfy the forward noncollinearity condition.
\item \textbf{(Nonzero forward edge.)} The forward direct slope satisfies \(\gamma\ne 0\).
\end{itemize}

47156b54cb3ac3056915bef1e1cd50f40f41376e.title: Reverse class \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\)
47156b54cb3ac3056915bef1e1cd50f40f41376e.body:
Let \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\) be the set of all probability laws \(P\in \operatorname{Laws}(\mathbb R^2)\) for which there exist centered latent source variables
\[
S_0,\ldots,S_{m+1}
\]
such that:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Source independence.)} The sources \(S_0,\ldots,S_{m+1}\) are mutually independent.
\item \textbf{(Finite cumulant order.)} Each source has finite moments through order \(K\).
\item \textbf{(Source non-Gaussianity.)} Each source is non-Gaussian.
\item \textbf{(Reverse representation.)} The bivariate observed outcome vector \((X,Y)^\top\) admits the reverse \(Y\to X\) latent linear non-Gaussian representation with loading vectors \(v_j\).
\item \textbf{(Reverse noncollinearity.)} The reverse direct and latent finite slopes \(\delta,\sigma_1,\ldots,\sigma_m\) satisfy the reverse noncollinearity condition.
\item \textbf{(Nonzero reverse edge.)} The reverse direct slope satisfies \(\delta\ne 0\).
\end{itemize}

018b34e93fc9e58857043af950a6f2046f990c96.title: Truncated cumulant vector \(T_L(P)\)
018b34e93fc9e58857043af950a6f2046f990c96.body:
For \(P\in \operatorname{Laws}(\mathbb R^2)\), define
\[
T_L(P)=\bigl(\kappa_{r,a}(P):2\le r\le L,\ 0\le a\le r\bigr),
\]
where
\[
\kappa_{r,a}(P)
=
\operatorname{Cum}_P(\underbrace{X,\ldots,X}_{r-a\ \mathrm{copies}},
\underbrace{Y,\ldots,Y}_{a\ \mathrm{copies}}).
\]
The coordinate dimension is
\[
q_L=\frac{L(L+3)}{2}-2.
\]

381dca6e0774d2bcf1ae0e12e6ef54263c01998d.title: Forward cumulant map \(\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\)
381dca6e0774d2bcf1ae0e12e6ef54263c01998d.body:
The forward cumulant map \(\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\) is defined coordinatewise by
\[
\bigl[\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(\gamma,\rho,c)\bigr]_{r,a}
=
\sum_{j=0}^{m+1}
c_{jr}\,(u_{j1})^{r-a}(u_{j2})^a,
\qquad
2\le r\le L,\quad 0\le a\le r,
\]
where
\[
u_0=(1,\gamma),\qquad
u_j=(1,\rho_j)\ \text{for }1\le j\le m,\qquad
u_{m+1}=(0,1).
\]

e3ac677d60d538be4b5371be39efb696530c4813.title: Reverse cumulant map \(\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\)
e3ac677d60d538be4b5371be39efb696530c4813.body:
The reverse cumulant map \(\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\) is defined coordinatewise by
\[
\bigl[\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(\delta,\sigma,d)\bigr]_{r,a}
=
\sum_{j=0}^{m+1}
d_{jr}\,(v_{j1})^{r-a}(v_{j2})^a,
\qquad
2\le r\le L,\quad 0\le a\le r,
\]
where
\[
v_0=(1,0),\qquad
v_j=(\sigma_j,1)\ \text{for }1\le j\le m,\qquad
v_{m+1}=(\delta,1).
\]

eb1214be7e9be93dbfbd1faee4c6e94a58f4eb21.title: Image varieties \(C^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\) and \(C^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\)

90f40f56fc9a7a6a6887be0732768a9aa4b95028.title: Generic loci \(\Theta^{\mathrm{right},\circ}_{m,L}\) and \(\Theta^{\mathrm{left},\circ}_{m,L}\)
90f40f56fc9a7a6a6887be0732768a9aa4b95028.body:
The forward generic parameter locus is
\[
\Theta^{\mathrm{right},\circ}_{m,L}
=
\left\{
(\gamma,\rho,c)\in \Theta^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}:
\gamma
\prod_{i=1}^m(\gamma-\rho_i)
\prod_{1\le i<\ell\le m}(\rho_i-\rho_\ell)
\prod_{j=0}^{m+1}\prod_{r=2}^L c_{jr}
\ne 0
\right\}.
\]
The reverse generic parameter locus is
\[
\Theta^{\mathrm{left},\circ}_{m,L}
=
\left\{
(\delta,\sigma,d)\in \Theta^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}:
\delta
\prod_{i=1}^m(\delta-\sigma_i)
\prod_{1\le i<\ell\le m}(\sigma_i-\sigma_\ell)
\prod_{j=0}^{m+1}\prod_{r=2}^L d_{jr}
\ne 0
\right\}.
\]

1efd483cd139c9664ab566fdb12363c0af8c8c1d.title: Fibers \(R^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(t)\) and \(R^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(t)\)
1efd483cd139c9664ab566fdb12363c0af8c8c1d.body:
For a cumulant vector \(t\), the forward and reverse fibers are
\[
R^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(t)
=
\left\{
\theta\in \Theta^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}:
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}(\theta)=t
\right\},
\qquad
R^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(t)
=
\left\{
\eta\in \Theta^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}:
\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}(\eta)=t
\right\}.
\]

6172b3a5b504b7728faabba97a4d3613a4b47b81.title: Admissible source swaps \(G_m\)
6172b3a5b504b7728faabba97a4d3613a4b47b81.body:
For \(\pi\in G_m\), the forward action on parameters is
\[
\pi\cdot(\gamma,(\rho_i)_{i=1}^m,(c_{jr})_{j,r})
=
(\gamma,(\rho_{\pi(i)})_{i=1}^m,(c'_{jr})_{j,r}),
\]
where
\[
c'_{ir}=c_{\pi(i),r}\quad(1\le i\le m),\qquad
c'_{0r}=c_{0r},\qquad
c'_{m+1,r}=c_{m+1,r}.
\]
The left action is the analogous action with \(\gamma,\rho,c\) replaced by \(\delta,\sigma,d\). Thus the source labels \(0\) and \(m+1\) are fixed, while the labels \(1,\ldots,m\) are permuted.

777c7362ca6cdb4351f39eb882624df85baf700a.title: Generic full-fiber compatibility \(E_m\)
777c7362ca6cdb4351f39eb882624df85baf700a.body:
Define
\[
E_m
=
\left\{
t\in \mathbb C^{q_K}:
\bigl(R^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}(t)\cap \Theta^{\mathrm{right},\circ}_{m,K}\neq\varnothing
\ \text{and}\ 
R^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}(t)\neq\varnothing\bigr)
\ \text{or}\
\bigl(R^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}(t)\cap \Theta^{\mathrm{left},\circ}_{m,K}\neq\varnothing
\ \text{and}\ 
R^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}(t)\neq\varnothing\bigr)
\right\}.
\]
Let
\[
\overline E_m=\operatorname{ZariskiClosure}(E_m).
\]
The corresponding exceptional preimages are
\[
H^{\mathrm{right}}_m
=
\Theta^{\mathrm{right},\circ}_{m,K}
\cap
\bigl(\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\bigr)^{-1}(E_m),
\qquad
H^{\mathrm{left}}_m
=
\Theta^{\mathrm{left},\circ}_{m,K}
\cap
\bigl(\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\bigr)^{-1}(E_m).
\]

fcf976cc6d843da425db0a51b81b6c93817cacb3.title: Real feasible regions \(F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\) and \(F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\)
fcf976cc6d843da425db0a51b81b6c93817cacb3.body:
The forward real feasible region \(F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,L}\) is the set of all real tuples \((\gamma,\rho,c)\in \mathbb R^{m+1}\times \mathbb R^{n(L-1)}\) such that \(\gamma\neq 0\), the slopes \(\rho_1,\ldots,\rho_m,\gamma\) are pairwise distinct, and, for every \(0\leq j\leq m+1\), there exists a centered non-Gaussian real random variable \(S_j\) with \(\mathbb E|S_j|^L<\infty\) satisfying
\[
\operatorname{Cum}(\underbrace{S_j,\ldots,S_j}_{r\ \mathrm{times}})=c_{jr},
\qquad 2\leq r\leq L.
\]
The reverse real feasible region \(F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,L}\) is defined analogously, with \((\delta,\sigma,d)\) in place of \((\gamma,\rho,c)\) and with \(\delta\neq 0\).

daf3bd59e216bcc71ca3a0edaeff4024ec21d7a2.title: Information order \(K^\star(m)\)
daf3bd59e216bcc71ca3a0edaeff4024ec21d7a2.body:
Define \(K^\star(m)\) to be the minimum integer \(L\geq 2\), if such an integer exists, such that for each \(b\in\{\mathrm{right},\mathrm{left}\}\), outside a proper real algebraic subset of \(F^b_{m,L}\), the cumulant vector
\[
\Phi^b_{m,L}(\theta)
\]
has no representation in
\[
F^{\operatorname{opposite}(b)}_{m,L}.
\]
If no such \(L\) exists, set \(K^\star(m)=\infty\).

5e8548feff39e0a1291a87d144877ac905b40c4b.title: Feasible-fiber predicate \(\operatorname{FeasFiber}^{b}_m(t)\)
5e8548feff39e0a1291a87d144877ac905b40c4b.body:
For \(b\in\{\mathrm{right},\mathrm{left}\}\), define \(\operatorname{FeasFiber}^{b}_m(t)\) to be the atomic-certificate predicate asserting that there exist real \(b\)-loading and source-cumulant coordinates \(\lambda\), and, for each \(0\leq j\leq m+1\), witnesses
\[
(w_{jh},z_{jh})_{h=1}^{m+2},
\]
such that
\[
\Phi^b_{m,K}(\lambda)=t,
\]
the direct slope for \(b\) is nonzero, all finite loading slopes for \(b\) are pairwise distinct, and, writing \(k_{jr}\) for the source cumulants contained in \(\lambda\) and
\[
\mu_{jr}=B_r(0,k_{j2},\ldots,k_{jr}),
\]
one has
\[
\sum_{h=1}^{m+2} w_{jh}=1,\qquad
w_{jh}\geq 0,\qquad
\sum_{h=1}^{m+2} w_{jh}z_{jh}^r=\mu_{jr}\quad(1\leq r\leq K),
\qquad
\mu_{j2}>0.
\]
The second component is the separate assertion that there is a real quantifier-elimination, sign-invariant cylindrical algebraic decomposition decision procedure with variable order \(t\), then \(\lambda\), then the witnesses, together with an apolar rank-open support-annihilator decision certificate for the fixed axis \(b\). This definition does not include the equivalence between \(\operatorname{FeasFiber}^{b}_m(t)\) and nonemptiness of the corresponding real-source feasible fiber.

aaed804127a5357ddd2ff9e2bbb9663df6aa8958.title: Separated model domain \(M^{\mathrm{sep}}_{m,K}\)
aaed804127a5357ddd2ff9e2bbb9663df6aa8958.body:
Define \(M^{\mathrm{sep}}_{m,K}\) to be the class of structural models \(M\) such that either \(M\) has a forward representation with parameter \(\theta\in F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\) and
\[
R^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\bigl(T_K(P_M)\bigr)\cap F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}=\varnothing,
\]
or \(M\) has a reverse representation with parameter \(\eta\in F^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}\) and
\[
R^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}\bigl(T_K(P_M)\bigr)\cap F^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}=\varnothing.
\]

7bf47598b3e0a77d5f3975f5645b0d2ba9445af5.title: Worked incidence systems \(A_1\) and \(A_2\)
7bf47598b3e0a77d5f3975f5645b0d2ba9445af5.body:
For \(m=1\) and \(K=4\), \(A_1\) is the \(12\)-equation incidence system
\[
t_{r,a}
=
c_{0r}\gamma^a+c_{1r}\rho^a+c_{2r}\mathbf 1_{\{a=r\}}
=
d_{0r}\mathbf 1_{\{a=0\}}+d_{1r}\sigma^{r-a}+d_{2r}\delta^{r-a},
\qquad
r=2,3,4,\quad 0\leq a\leq r,
\]
together with the generic-locus disjunction. Its common-axis subfamily is given by
\[
\rho=\sigma=0,\qquad
\delta\gamma=1,\qquad
d_{0r}=c_{1r},\qquad
d_{1r}=c_{2r},\qquad
d_{2r}=c_{0r}\gamma^r.
\]

For \(m=2\) and \(K=6\), \(A_2\) is the \(25\)-equation incidence system
\[
t_{r,a}
=
c_{0r}\gamma^a+c_{1r}\rho_1^a+c_{2r}\rho_2^a+c_{3r}\mathbf 1_{\{a=r\}}
=
d_{0r}\mathbf 1_{\{a=0\}}+d_{1r}\sigma_1^{r-a}+d_{2r}\sigma_2^{r-a}+d_{3r}\delta^{r-a},
\qquad
r=2,\ldots,6,\quad 0\leq a\leq r,
\]
together with the generic-locus disjunction. Its common-axis subfamily is given by
\[
\rho_1=\sigma_1=0,\qquad
\delta\gamma=1,\qquad
\sigma_2\rho_2=1,\qquad
d_{0r}=c_{1r},\qquad
d_{1r}=c_{3r},\qquad
d_{2r}=c_{2r}\rho_2^r,\qquad
d_{3r}=c_{0r}\gamma^r.
\]

883d4048566395bbe5ff007df6b346137bc6a8df.title: Quotient-fiber comparison
883d4048566395bbe5ff007df6b346137bc6a8df.body:
The separation handle is the algebraic comparison of simultaneous binary-form decompositions
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}(\theta)=\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}(\eta)
\]
after quotienting each same-arrow fiber by the admissible source-label permutation group \(G_m\).  The comparison uses the axis directions fixed in the two cumulant maps and the Jacobian ranks of the resulting quotient-fiber equations.

d7ac0987b588ac75484e2c4d1d09457e052b660d.title: \(K_-=2m+1\) twin construction proposition
d7ac0987b588ac75484e2c4d1d09457e052b660d.body:
The real twin construction handle is the proposition that there exist real forward and reverse parameters \(\theta\) and \(\eta\), each lying in the compactly supported feasible region through order
\[
K_-=2m+1,
\]
such that their truncated cumulant maps agree through order \(K_-\):
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K_-}(\theta)=\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K_-}(\eta).
\]
This definition records only the existence proposition; it does not include a construction or proof.

8ee3e511547e26f9dcb84258a9eeff7bdef6bbd3.title: Cylindrical atlas interface
8ee3e511547e26f9dcb84258a9eeff7bdef6bbd3.body: The real atlas handle is the following external classical interface.  For every finite family of real polynomials and every variable order, there exists an adapted cylindrical algebraic decomposition that is sign-invariant for the generated projection family, is obtained by recursive section and sector lifting, decides polynomial sign conditions cellwise, and supports Tarski--Seidenberg semialgebraic projection.  This is the classical existence-and-structure interface of \citet[Section~2.3 and Theorem~2.2.1]{BochnakCosteRoy1998} and \citet[Chapter~5]{BasuPollackRoy2006}; it is paper-agnostic and carries no algorithmic, computability, payload, or complexity claim.  It is distinct from the exact rational Gröbner--CAD interface.

4bcad92f1804fe8741f13c21349dff0e08ef18d9.title: Exact Real Atlas
4bcad92f1804fe8741f13c21349dff0e08ef18d9.body: A natural next question is whether, for each fixed \(m\), one can construct a finite computable semialgebraic cylindrical atlas of the real exceptional closure such that, on each base cell, the complete feasible forward and reverse fibers are finite unions of recursively cylindrical section and sector cells and their nonemptiness labels are constant.  Such an atlas would also have to include singular and nongeneric boundary branches and decide exactly when both real arrows are feasible.  Establishing this would require a paper-specific CAD and elimination construction beyond the external cylindrical-atlas interface.

389738b65daaa75bf124c8047d76f6338346c9c6.title: Exact Real Exceptional Atlas
389738b65daaa75bf124c8047d76f6338346c9c6.body: A natural future direction, building on Definition~\ref{obj:def:generic-full-fiber-compatibility}, is to determine whether, for each fixed \(m\), the real exceptional closure \(\overline E_m\) admits a finite computable semialgebraic cylindrical atlas with the following exactness property: on every base cell, the complete feasible forward and reverse fibers decompose into finite unions of recursively cylindrical section and sector cells, with constant nonemptiness labels. Such an atlas would have to include singular and nongeneric boundary branches and would decide exactly when both real arrows are feasible; constructing it would require substantial CAD and elimination machinery beyond the present algebraic setup.

9e3681e2bbef3d444f234824abb9f384f6bdc1c0.title: Exceptional-closure ideal \(J_m\)
9e3681e2bbef3d444f234824abb9f384f6bdc1c0.body:
Let \(K=2m+2\). The ideal
\[
J_m\subset \mathbb C[t_{r,a}:2\le r\le K,\ 0\le a\le r]
\]
is the exceptional-closure ideal if it is the intersection of the two \(t\)-elimination ideals obtained from the simultaneous incidence equations
\[
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}(\theta)=t=\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}(\eta),
\]
after saturating once by the right genericity product and once by the left genericity product. For a cumulant vector \(t\), the notation \(J_m(t)=0\) means that every polynomial in the ideal \(J_m\) vanishes at \(t\). Equivalently, the zero set of \(J_m\) is the exceptional closure \(\overline E_m\) in the finite cumulant-coordinate space.

6ee5f475774dea3b4a1b3c0bacfa2102404a0274.title: Semialgebraic stack \(\Gamma_b\)
6ee5f475774dea3b4a1b3c0bacfa2102404a0274.body: Let \(K=2m+2\) and let \(b\in\{\mathrm{right},\mathrm{left}\}\). For a cumulant vector \(t\) and a real loading-and-source-cumulant coordinate \(\lambda\), the relation \((t,\lambda)\in\Gamma_b\) holds exactly when \(J_m(t)=0\), meaning that every polynomial in the ideal \(J_m\) vanishes at \(t\); \(\Phi^b_{m,K}(\lambda)=t\); the direct \(b\)-slope in \(\lambda\) is nonzero; the finite \(b\)-loading slopes in \(\lambda\) are pairwise distinct; and \(Q_K\) holds for every source cumulant list carried by \(\lambda\).

e906e15293c7e00d70859303c5904c494d868c03.title: Semialgebraic stack \(\Gamma_b\)
e906e15293c7e00d70859303c5904c494d868c03.body:
Let \(K=2m+2\), let \(b\in\{\mathrm{right},\mathrm{left}\}\), and use the branch convention
\[
\Phi^b_{m,K}=
\begin{cases}
\Phi^{\mathrm{right}}_{m,K}, & b=\mathrm{right},\\
\Phi^{\mathrm{left}}_{m,K}, & b=\mathrm{left}.
\end{cases}
\]
For a cumulant vector \(t\) and a real loading-and-source-cumulant coordinate \(\lambda\), the relation \((t,\lambda)\in\Gamma_b\) holds exactly when:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Exceptional equations.)} \(J_m(t)=0\).
\item \textbf{(Cumulant parametrization.)} \(\Phi^b_{m,K}(\lambda)=t\).
\item \textbf{(Nonzero direct slope.)} The direct \(b\)-slope in \(\lambda\) is nonzero.
\item \textbf{(Distinct finite slopes.)} The finite \(b\)-loading slopes in \(\lambda\) are pairwise distinct.
\item \textbf{(Atomic source certificates.)} \(Q_K\) holds for every source cumulant list carried by \(\lambda\).
\end{itemize}
